rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference visible ports and features.
Use component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component types or implementations.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Validate that AGREE expressions align with AADL data types to prevent type mismatch errors.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or unit tokens directly into AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should match the data type of the referenced port or feature.
Do not include implementation-specific paths in component type annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference opaque Boolean or enumeration data as numeric values in AGREE.
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
